topics.topic: When Algorithms Miss: The Human Cost of AI Diagnostics
topics.description: Drawing on 30+ years of ER experience, Dr. Camps exposes the critical gaps between what AI diagnostic tools promise and what actually happens when patients arrive in crisis. He offers a frontline framework for building AI systems that augment rather than replace clinical judgment.
topics.audience: Health tech founders, clinical AI developers, and hospital innovation officers

topics.topic: Interoperability or Bust: Why ER Data Is Still Broken
topics.description: The emergency room is the ultimate stress test for health data exchange, and it still fails patients daily. Dr. Camps translates the chaos of real-time trauma care into a clear indictment of fragmented health IT infrastructure and what fixing it actually requires.
topics.audience: Health IT leaders, EHR vendors, and CMS ecosystem stakeholders

topics.topic: Burnout at the Bedside: Technology's Role in the Clinician Crisis
topics.description: Health tech promised to reduce clinician burden, yet ER physicians are leaving medicine faster than ever. Dr. Camps delivers an unflinching look at how poorly designed technology contributes to frontline burnout and what human-centered design must fix.
topics.audience: Health tech developers, hospital HR and operations leaders, and workforce strategists

discussion_points: When Algorithms Miss: The Human Cost of AI Diagnostics
Interoperability or Bust: Why ER Data Is Still Broken
Burnout at the Bedside: Technology's Role in the Clinician Crisis
bio: 30+ years frontline ER experience. Published author of Never Let Them See You Sweat. Extensive media presence across TV, print, and digital.
